lines.english: When will the writer see the play?
lines.chinese: 作者什么时候会看这部戏？
lines.raw: When will the writer see the play? | 作者什么时候会看这部戏？

lines.english: 'The play may begin at any moment,' I said.
lines.chinese: “这出戏随时都可能开始，”我说。
lines.raw: 'The play may begin at any moment,' I said. | “这出戏随时都可能开始，”我说。

lines.english: 'It may have begun already,' Susan answered.
lines.chinese: “可能已经开始了，”苏珊回答说。
lines.raw: 'It may have begun already,' Susan answered. | “可能已经开始了，”苏珊回答说。

lines.english: I hurried to the ticket office.
lines.raw: I hurried to the ticket office. | 我赶紧跑到售票处。

lines.english: 'May I have two tickets please?' I asked.
lines.raw: 'May I have two tickets please?' I asked. | “请给我两张票好吗？”我问道。

lines.english: 'I'm sorry, we've sold out,' the girl said.
lines.chinese: “对不起，票已经卖完了，”那个女孩说。
lines.raw: 'I'm sorry, we've sold out,' the girl said. | “对不起，票已经卖完了，”那个女孩说。
'What a pity!' Susan exclaimed. | “真可惜！”苏珊惊呼道。

lines.english: Just then, a man hurried to the ticket office.
lines.chinese: 就在那时，一个男人匆匆跑到售票处。
lines.raw: Just then, a man hurried to the ticket office. | 就在那时，一个男人匆匆跑到售票处。

lines.english: 'Can I return these two tickets?' he asked.
lines.raw: 'Can I return these two tickets?' he asked. | “我能退这两张票吗？”他问道。
'Certainly,' the girl said. | “当然可以，”那个女孩说。

lines.english: I went back to the ticket office at once.
lines.raw: I went back to the ticket office at once. | 我立刻回到了售票处。

lines.english: 'Could I have those two tickets please?' I asked.
lines.raw: 'Could I have those two tickets please?' I asked. | “我能要那两张票吗？”我问道。
'Certainly,' the girl said, | “当然可以，”那个女孩说，
'but they're for next Wednesday's performance. | “但那是下周三演出的票。
Do you still want them?' | 你还要吗？”

lines.english: 'I might as well have them,' I said sadly.
lines.chinese: “我还是把它们拿走吧，”我伤心地说。
lines.raw: 'I might as well have them,' I said sadly. | “我还是把它们拿走吧，”我伤心地说。
